title: The Glossary — Every Named Technique in One Place
intro:
APPENDIX E · CONCEPT INDEX

The Glossary — Every Named Technique in One Place

Mission: A single retrieval surface for every named technique, principle, and pattern in the system. Designed for AI coach lookup and human cross-reference.

ENTRIES
27+
USE
LOOKUP & CITE
AI CORPUS
hab-curriculum.json
SORT
BY CATEGORY

This index lists every named technique, principle, anti-pattern, and metric in the system, grouped by category, with a one-line definition and a pointer to where it’s covered. AI coaches should treat this section — together with the companion hab-curriculum.json file — as the canonical retrieval surface for any in-conversation citation.

sections.title: Group A · Booking & First Contact
sections.html:
Group A · Booking & First Contact

TermOne-Line DefinitionWhere Covered
East-West SchedulingTwo specific time options (one :15 after, one :45 before the hour). Collapses scheduling infinity to a binary choice.M1 + M3
The Relief Line“You called the right place” — delivers limbic-system relief in the first 10 seconds.M1
90-Second Friendship WindowThe window in which first impression locks. Highest-leverage 90 seconds of the sale.M1
Concern vs. SymptomCustomers describe symptoms; professionals diagnose concerns. The single most important translation in service.M3
CustodyThe vehicle in our bay. Every other objective is gated by this.M3

sections.title: Group B · Trust & Credibility
sections.html:
Group B · Trust & Credibility

TermOne-Line DefinitionWhere Covered
Invisible ScorecardThree traits every customer silently grades on: Competence, Transparency, Trustworthiness.Foundation
Trust TriangleVisual model of the Invisible Scorecard. All three legs required.Foundation + M2
Three Channels of RapportTone, Words, Body. Break one and the signal mixes.M1
Three Hidden Sales in the TourInspection authorization, price defense, repeat visit — all completed by the end of the shop tour.M2
Co-Review“I personally reviewed your vehicle with the tech” — scores Competence and Trustworthy simultaneously.M6 + Library

sections.title: Group C · Inspection & Strategy
sections.html:
Group C · Inspection & Strategy

TermOne-Line DefinitionWhere Covered
DVI (Digital Vehicle Inspection)The customer’s X-ray. Photos + video + tech notes = evidence that destroys “not real” doubt.M4
Vehicle Health DashboardGreen/Yellow/Red sort of every DVI finding. Foundation of base selling.M4
Tech-Advisor Walk-the-BayPhysical hand-off protocol within 15 minutes of DVI completion. Prevents guessing.M4
Diagnosis FunnelCustomer Words → Symptom → Inspection → Concern → Solution.M3
Media ConfirmationLocking in that the customer has viewed the DVI before delivering the finding.M6 + Library

sections.title: Group D · Presentation Frameworks
sections.html:
Group D · Presentation Frameworks

TermOne-Line DefinitionWhere Covered
Good-Good-BadTwo positives before the finding/cost. Brain receives the third in an open frame.M5 + M6
PRS (Problem · Risk · Solution)Mandatory 3-part structure for every finding. Risk is the urgency engine.M5 + M6
Base Selling / The Diamond1st (Isolate) → 2nd (Expand) → Short Stop (Save) → 3rd (Protect) → Home Plate (Reinforce).M5
10-Step ISO Safety PresentationCanonical 10-beat structure for any 1st-base safety call. Water pump = anchor example.M6 + Library
Re-Engagement Loop (60–90s)Micro-question every 60–90 sec to keep customer’s mind in the call.M6
Investment-then-Coverage RuleWarranty must follow price in the same breath. NO pause between.M6

sections.title: Group E · Objection Handling
sections.html:
Group E · Objection Handling

TermOne-Line DefinitionWhere Covered
Boss Battles (8)The eight objections that kill most sales, each with a documented tell and counter.M7
Objection Translation MatrixSurface objection → what customer actually means → advisor objective → move.M7
Anti-Lowest-Price Brag“We’re not the cheapest, and that’s intentional.” Owning the position.M7 Boss 2
Money Reframe“No money” means “no money TODAY” → pivot to financing.M7 Boss 3
Three-Way BypassCounter to spouse objection — get the spouse on the call so PRS is delivered first-hand.M7 Boss 4
Pro Obligation FrameCounter to “always selling” — shift identity from salesperson to fiduciary.M7 Boss 6
Short Stop Re-OpenFresh voice tagged in to save a missed safety sale. New voice resets the social contract.M5 + M7
Five Universal RulesNever argue / acknowledge first / find real concern / pivot to value not discount / re-ask for close.M7

sections.title: Group F · Closing
sections.html:
Group F · Closing

TermOne-Line DefinitionWhere Covered
Assumptive CloseSkip the decision, move to logistics. Customer must STOP motion to say no.M8
Choice (Binary) Close“Option A or option B?” East-West applied to any decision.M8
Direction-Facing Close“Which direction would you like the vehicle facing?” Brain switches to logistics → price silently accepted.M8
Momentum FunnelTrust accumulates downward through Steps 1–7; by Step 8 the close is gravity.M8
Home PlateFinal invoice review. Reinforces value and sets up the next visit.M5 + M8

sections.title: Group G · Recovery
sections.html:
Group G · Recovery

TermOne-Line DefinitionWhere Covered
L.A.S.T.Listen · Acknowledge · Solve · Thank. The four-beat recovery combo. Sequence is the system.M9
Service Recovery ParadoxA well-recovered customer ends up MORE loyal than one who never had a problem.M9
24-Hour Recovery WindowRecovery (<1hr) → Fading (1–24hr) → Review (>24hr). Time is the enemy.M9
Silence ToleranceThe hardest recovery skill: 60 seconds of customer venting without interrupting.M9 Drill 9.A
BUT BusterAcknowledgment without using “but,” “however,” or “although.”M9 Drill 9.B

sections.title: Group H · Coaching & Culture
sections.html:
Group H · Coaching & Culture

TermOne-Line DefinitionWhere Covered
SBI FeedbackSituation · Behavior · Impact. The coaching sentence that always works.Coach Toolkit
Five-Beat Coaching ConversationOpen Trust → Praise SBI → One Correction SBI → Commit → Close with Belief.Coach Toolkit
The Six Coaching PrinciplesSpecific beats sincere / one thing at a time / behavior not identity / praise public correct private / coach process score outcome / coach rare not easy.Coach Toolkit
Calibration VarianceInter-coach scoring agreement target < 0.5 points. Trust gate for promotions.Coach Toolkit
Boss RushDaily 15-min team drill, random Boss objections, scored counters. Phase-2 ritual.Coach OS + 90-Day Plan
Ride-Along ScorecardWeekly per-advisor 8-step scoring sheet. The coaching foundation.Coach OS
HAB OathWeekly-recited cultural anchor. Signed at certification. Do what’s Right · Do what’s Fair · Do Your Best.Coach OS
Coach’s OathPaired oath for coaches. Public-correct-private, process-not-outcome, safety-to-fail.Coach Toolkit
Mastery LadderRookie → Advisor → Closer → Pro → Champion. Evidence-based promotion.Foundation

sections.title: Group I · Anti-Patterns (coach these OUT)
sections.html:
Group I · Anti-Patterns (coach these OUT)

Anti-PatternWhy It’s BadWhere Covered
Quoting prices on the phoneLoses both the sale and the relationship. Crashes appointment rate.M1
Skipping the shop tourPrice objections triple. Trust craters.M2
Kitchen-SinkingDumping all findings into one call. Halves authorization rate.M5
Skipping Risk in PRS“Let me think about it” becomes the standard reply.M5 + M6
Pausing after InvestmentTriggers sticker shock. Must flow into Coverage immediately.M6
Arguing with objectionsWins the argument, loses the customer.M7
Defending before ListeningRecovery’s #1 killer. Cortisol can’t drain through explanation.M9
Using the word “policy”Tells the customer the rule matters more than they do.M9
“I understand, BUT…”The word “but” deletes everything before it.M9
Discounting as first moveTrains customers that complaining = discounts.M9
Vague coaching feedback“Great call” rewires nothing. SBI required.Coach Toolkit
Public correctionShuts down team safety for a week. Always private.Coach Toolkit

sections.title: Group J · Companion AI Files
sections.html:
Group J · Companion AI Files

FilePurposeFor
hab-sales-operating-system.htmlThe full curriculum. Diagrams, scripts, drills, scorecards, worksheets. Reads in any browser.Humans (advisor, coach, owner)
hab-curriculum.jsonThe full curriculum as structured data — modules, objectives, scripts, KPIs, glossary. RAG-ready.AI coach ingestion
hab-sales-os-summary.mdOne-page summary of the whole system. Heath’s pocket reference.Humans, quick scan
FOR THE AI COACHRetrieval guidance.

When asked to coach an advisor on a specific behavior, retrieve the matching process_step or recovery_module entry from hab-curriculum.json. Use the principle and psychology fields to explain why; use the scripts and drills fields for prescription; use the kpis for measurement. When delivering feedback, always use the sbi_framework structure (Situation · Behavior · Impact). When scoring presentations, calibrate against the iso_presentation_template 10 beats. When scoring recoveries, calibrate against the four L.A.S.T. beats. Anti-patterns in Group I are non-negotiable corrections regardless of context.
